output.revisionContext: Revision 1 -- block updated to show ALL topic pills (multiple pills possible) and use sprintf for author/date. Verifying: (1) multi-pill flex-wrap layout, (2) author/date text renders the same, (3) overall design still matches Figma.

variationDescriptions: Default - Page Accent Color (neon-green gradient, single STUDENT LIFE pill, no excerpt)
With Excerpt Shown (neon-green gradient, single pill, excerpt visible below title)
Topic Accent Color Override (blue gradient from topic accent, single pill, no excerpt)
Full Options - Excerpt + Topic Color (blue gradient, single pill, excerpt visible)

observations: Overall shape and proportions match Figma design -- full-width hero section with background image fading via mask gradient
Content is left-aligned in the left portion of the viewport, consistent with Figma
Topic pill, title, author/date meta stack vertically in correct order
Gradient overlay transitions from image at top to accent-colored gradient at bottom -- matches Figma pattern
Visual weight and density match the Figma reference
No content overflow or clipping observed at any breakpoint
PHP template (PostHero.php line 70-86) uses foreach loop over all $categories, rendering a pill for each
TSX editor (PostHero.tsx line 189-213) uses allCategories.map() to render all category pills
Pills container uses 'flex flex-wrap gap-2' (PHP line 71, TSX line 188) which provides 8px gap between pills and supports wrapping when multiple pills overflow the row
Each pill has 'shrink-0 whitespace-nowrap' to prevent pill text from wrapping or shrinking -- pills maintain readable size and wrap to next row instead
Figma pill container (node 669:33750) uses 'flex flex-wrap' with gap-y-[8px] for wrapping -- implementation gap-2 (8px) matches the vertical wrapping gap

Test post currently has only one category ('Student Life') assigned, so only one pill renders. This is correct behavior -- the code iterates all assigned categories. With multiple categories, pills would render side-by-side and wrap as needed
Single pill renders identically to the previous revision -- no visual regression
PHP uses sprintf with translatable strings: sprintf(__('By: %s', 'takt'), $postAuthor) on line 110 and sprintf(__('Published on: %s', 'takt'), $postDate) on line 122
TSX uses @wordpress/i18n sprintf: sprintf(__('By: %s', 'takt'), postAuthor) on line 241 and sprintf(__('Published on: %s', 'takt'), postDate) on line 249
Rendered output at 1440px shows 'By: leandro' and 'Published on: 13 February 2026' -- format is identical to the pre-revision format
Figma reference shows 'By: Lubna Patel' and 'Published on: October 2, 2025' -- same 'By: {name}' / 'Published on: {date}' pattern, only content differs due to test data
sprintf is transparent to the visual output -- renders identically to string concatenation
Overall layout, spacing, typography, colors, and gradient all match the Figma reference and the previous passing QA
No visual regression introduced by the multi-pill or sprintf changes
Full-width section with relative positioning -- matches Figma
hero-inner container with background image layer + content layer structure -- matches Figma Hero pattern
Content constrained to max-w-[700px] mr-auto (left-aligned, max 700px) -- matches Figma content width of 687px inner + 32px padding
Container utility provides correct horizontal centering: at 1440px viewport, container side margin matches Figma's 32px + 32px padding
Background image fills full section with w-full h-full object-cover
Mask gradient (black 40% to transparent 100%) fades image progressively -- matches Figma
Both -webkit-mask-image and mask-image applied for cross-browser support
Image renders correctly across all three browsers at all breakpoints
top-gradient div renders the accent-colored gradient overlay
Default variation uses page accent color (neon-green) -- gradient appears as yellow-green blend matching Figma
useTopicColor variations correctly switch to blue gradient from topic accent color
Gradient extends below the hero section appropriately
Pill renders with rounded-full shape, visible border, light background -- proportions match Figma
Figma: p-[8px]. Implementation: px-2 py-2 (8px all around) -- EXACT MATCH
Figma: 14px Roboto Medium uppercase, leading 1.1. Implementation: text-sm font-medium uppercase leading-[1.1] -- EXACT MATCH
Figma: bg #eeffb5 (accent-lighter), border #ddff6a (accent). Implementation: bg-accent-lighter border-accent -- tokens map correctly
Pill text is charcoal via text-charcoal -- EXACT MATCH
Each pill scoped with its own accent color class when available -- correct per-pill coloring
Figma: Anton Regular 64px, line-height 1.1, charcoal
Implementation: text-header-0 (Anton Regular 64px at desktop), text-charcoal
Title renders in large Anton font at correct size -- NOT italic (correct, Anton appears slanted by design)
Line-height 1.2 vs Figma 1.1 accepted per planning decision DEC-003 (within tolerance)
Correctly hidden in variations 1 and 3 (showExcerpt=false)
Correctly visible in variations 2 and 4 (showExcerpt=true)
Uses text-body-large (18px Roboto) text-charcoal mt-6 (24px gap from title) -- matches Figma
Figma: Roboto Medium 18px, leading 1.2, charcoal. Implementation: text-lg font-medium leading-[1.2] text-charcoal -- EXACT MATCH
Format 'By: {name}' and 'Published on: {date}' matches Figma text pattern via sprintf
Meta group positioned with mt-12 (48px) below upper group -- matches Figma gap of 48px
Pill-to-title gap: Figma 24px, implementation gap-6 (24px) -- EXACT MATCH
Upper-to-lower group gap: Figma 48px, implementation mt-12 (48px) -- EXACT MATCH
Bottom padding desktop: Figma 64px, implementation sm:pb-16 (64px) -- EXACT MATCH
Bottom padding mobile: implementation pb-8 (32px) -- appropriate for mobile
All text uses text-charcoal (#1f1f1d) -- matches Figma charcoal color
Topic pill uses accent color system (bg-accent-lighter, border-accent) scoped via category accent class
Gradient uses accent color system via .hero class and .top-gradient CSS
No hardcoded hex values in implementation -- all use theme tokens
375px (mobile): Content stacks vertically, title wraps naturally, pill visible and correctly sized. Background image and mask gradient work correctly. Smaller bottom padding (pb-8 = 32px).
768px (tablet): Layout scales smoothly. Content constrained to max-width, gradient renders correctly. Larger bottom padding (sm:pb-16 = 64px) applies.
1024px (laptop): Full desktop layout, content left-aligned with max-width constraint. All elements proportionally correct.
1440px (desktop): Matches Figma reference. Full-width hero with correct proportions and spacing.
Chromium: Renders correctly at all 4 breakpoints (375, 768, 1024, 1440)
Firefox: Renders identically to Chromium -- mask-image gradient works correctly at all breakpoints
WebKit: Renders identically -- -webkit-mask-image fallback works correctly at all breakpoints
No cross-browser inconsistencies detected across any of the 12 screenshots
Variation 1 (Default): Page accent color gradient (neon-green), no excerpt, single pill -- renders correctly
Variation 2 (With Excerpt): Same gradient, excerpt visible between title and meta -- renders correctly
Variation 3 (Topic Color): Gradient switches to blue (topic accent color) -- useTopicColor toggle works correctly
Variation 4 (Full Options): Blue gradient + excerpt visible -- both options work together correctly

issues.recommendation: Accepted per planning decision DEC-003. The text-header-0 utility uses 1.2 line-height by default. Difference is within acceptable tolerance.

issues.figmaValue: Single text block with inherent line-height spacing (~3.6px between lines)
issues.recommendation: Implementation uses separate paragraph elements for semantic clarity (allows independent translatable prefixes via sprintf). Visual gap (4px vs ~3.6px) is imperceptible. No change needed.

checklist.hoverStates: N/A - no interactive elements in hero
checklist.focusStates: N/A - no focusable elements in hero
